rationale: Keep because the citing paper’s design explicitly uses product/time fixed effects plus an IV/BLUP-based correction for mean reversion and measurement error, while the cited paper is a methodological paper on fixed-effects inconsistency and IV/bias-correction for causal estimation. That methodological overlap suggests the citation may be serving as a substantive identification/estimation source rather than a generic background mention.
